content.problemStatement:
Nayra doesn't like stories of people receiving random arrays as birthday presents, but this time she received random arrays as presents for her own birthday! After struggling for a day trying to figure out what to do with the array, she asked Aryan for help. He gave her the following problem.

You are given 333 integers MMM, PPP and NNN.

For an array AAA, let f(A)f(A)f(A) be defined as the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence of AAA and let g(A)=(f(A))Pg(A) = (f(A))^Pg(A)=(f(A))P.

Consider all distinct arrays AAA of length NNN such that 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \le A_i \le M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N). Calculate the sum of g(A)g(A)g(A) over all such MNM^NMN arrays. Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 754974721754974721754974721.

Each test case has QQQ queries. For all QQQ queries, MMM and PPP remain the same, only NNN varies.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
First line of each test case contains three integers M,P,M, P,M,P, and QQQ.
Second line of each test case contains QQQ integers, values of NNN for each query.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line containing QQQ space-separated integers, answers for each query modulo 754974721754974721754974721.
